rulings.Case Title: NOEL CONSIGNY VS. WALTER WORSHAM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default And For Leave To File Answer And Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 15, 2016 line 1. DEFENDANT WALTER WORSHAM, MOISES MONGALO's Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default And For Leave To File Answer And Cross-Complaint GRANTED. No opposition filed. Answer and Cross-Complaint to be filed and served forthwith. Moving party prepare order. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BERTHA LUZ ALFARO VS. CYBERNIB CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 15, 2016 line 2. PLAINTIFF BERTHA ALFARO's DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT Plaintiff's demurrer is OFF CALENDAR, untimely per CCP 430.40. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice Ex. A is DENIED. The attached document is not a true and correct copy of the answer of file. Answer on file contains a proof of service executed by Defendants' counsel Andrew Cantor attesting to mail service on September 9, 2016. Plaintiff's Motion to Strike is GRANTED, no opposition filed. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice Exhibit B is GRANTED. Defendant Cybenib Corporation's Answer is Stricken due to Franchise Tax Board "forfeiture" status. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TESFAI MEKONNEN VS. DEREK PECK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 15, 2016 line 3. DEFENDANT DEREK PECK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Moving party failed to show that regulation 6.15A is applicable to the Lease agreement including addendum. Triable issues of fact regarding (1) applicability of SFRO sublet/assignment provisions re: Defendant's permanent residency at the premises and (2) landlord's knowledge of sublet. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: NEW LAND COMPANY VS. SAL GLYNN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 15, 2016 line 4. DEFENDANT SAL GLYNN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with 5-day leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith that attorney who executed the "3 Day Notice" does not "regularly" engage in consumer-debt-collection activity. Heintz v. Jenkins (1995) 514 U.S. 291. Plaintiff fails to explain how the definition of a "debt collector" in CC 1788.2 is applicable to the Federal Debt Collection Practices Act, especially in light of Heintz v. Jenkins (1995) 514 U.S. 291. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: HARLAN A SEE VS. ANTHONY ARSAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Of Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment; And Proposed Response; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 15, 2016 line 5. DEFENDANT ANTHONY ARSAN's Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment DENIED. No Proof of Service on file, no responsive pleading provided, no corroborating competent evidence. = (501/AJR)
